title: Haworthiopsis × lisbonensis
intro: Haworthiopsis × lisbonensis, a hybrid succulent plant, is a cross between Haworthiopsis fasciata and Haworthiopsis turgida. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which can aid in wound healing and skin issues. The bioactive constituents of Haworthiopsis × lisbonensis include flavonoids, terpenoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Haworthiopsis × lisbonensis is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, which are used to treat various skin conditions and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Haworthiopsis × lisbonensis is a hybrid succulent species within the family Asphodelaceae. It is believed to be a natural hybrid between Haworthia fasciata and Haworthia truncata. This classification places it within the Haworthia genus. However, it was reclassified as Haworthiopsis due to the reorganization of the Haworthia genus. This reclassification reflects the ongoing study and understanding of the relationships within the Asphodelaceae family.
